config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diletakkan di atas segalanya, itu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan dan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan dasar, dan perlindungan setiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States we have seen how easily "for the public good" can be used to excuse abuse, censorship, and discrimination. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since laws against violence, fraud, and public health threats exist for a reason. My view is that society should protect the common good by setting narrow limits on rights only when there is a clear, evidence-based necessity, not by treating the individual as secondary by default. When governments flip that hierarchy, ordinary people usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, mengabaikan minoritas, atau membenarkan kebijakan yang merugikan orang kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibangun lewat perlindungan hak dasar, aturan yang adil, dan pembatasan yang sangat ketat hanya saat benar-benar perlu. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua, yang paling sering terluka bukan “egoisme pribadi”, melainkan warga biasa yang tidak punya kuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle, because in the United States the phrase "for the common good" has too often been a cover for overreach, censorship, and discrimination. That said, I do not believe rights are unlimited; a society can and should set narrow, evidence-based limits for things like violence, fraud, and genuine public safety threats. What I reject is the idea that the individual is automatically secondary, because once that becomes the default, the people with the least power are the ones who get sacrificed. A healthy society protects the public good by protecting rights first, not by treating them as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan orang kecil, minoritas, atau kritik yang sah. Kepentingan umum memang ada dan kadang perlu membatasi hak tertentu, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat ketat, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan karena negara atau mayoritas merasa paling tahu. Justru kalau hak individu ditempatkan di bawah secara default, yang paling sering jadi korban adalah warga biasa yang tidak punya kuasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang melindungi hak setiap orang sambil menjaga kepentingan bersama secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society’s interests take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am wary of any slogan like "for the common good" because history here shows it can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular groups. At the same time, I do not believe rights are absolute in every case, since public safety, fraud prevention, and violence are real limits. My bottom line is that society is strongest when it protects individual rights first and only narrows them in rare, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
